rationale: Schema/type correctness holds throughout. Count *int semantics are correct and singly-homed: nil -> 1 via the exported CountValue() accessor, consumed by every reader (validation's ownSum/total loops, expandSlots, PanelGateExpectedReviewers). Repo-wide grep confirms the ONE out-of-package consumer (cmd/mindspec/config.go:153) was migrated off raw %d-on-pointer to CountValue(); cmd/mindspec/report.go's r.Count and cmd/mindspec/trace.go's s.Count are confirmed unrelated structs, untouched, as the plan asserts. No raw Count deref exists outside the two nil-guarded sites (config.go:129/132 inside CountValue, 545/546 inside a short-circuited nil check). No omitempty on the yaml tag, but Config is never re-marshaled (Load only calls yaml.Unmarshal), so this has no observable effect. YAML unmarshal edge cases are well covered: gates:{} vs absent, count-less entries -> nil Count -> CountValue()==1, and the mixed compact/exploded/lens fixture round-trips exactly, verified by running the suite myself. Deterministic ordering is solid: slot expansion walks a slice (no map iteration), the interleaved global cursor is correctly implemented (verified by hand-tracing both the falsifiability fixture and the 9-reviewer worked example against expandSlots, matching test expectations exactly), unknown-gate-key and substitutes validation sort map keys before use, and Bead-3-owned gate rendering already commits to PanelGateKeys enum order per the plan. Error wrapping is consistent with the pre-existing 109 convention (plain fmt.Errorf with recovery lines for user-facing guard errors; %w reserved for the two I/O-wrapping sites in loadUncached, both pre-existing and untouched). Ran go build ./..., go vet on both packages, and the full internal/config + cmd/mindspec suites including all six named bead-1 tests and TestConfigShow_EmitsPanelModelsLoop: all green, tree stayed porcelain-clean.
findings: Minor/non-blocking: no test exercises a Reviewer entry with BOTH Model and Family set to confirm the documented 'Model wins' precedence in the unexported model() accessor. The implementation is trivially correct by inspection (if r.Model != "" return r.Model; return r.Family), so this is a coverage gap, not a defect.

Confirmed by repo-wide grep: cmd/mindspec/report.go:172/236 (r.Count, a journal Report struct with plain int Count) and cmd/mindspec/trace.go:105 (s.Count, an unrelated trace-stats struct) are correctly out of scope for the pointerization — no missed consumer.

Verified DefaultConfig() leaves Gates/Note/Substitutes at Go zero values (nil map / empty string), consistent with the R2 'documented example, never the default' rule and the byte-identical-with-109 test.
